description: A word-level particle that marks additive focus, indicating that the element it precedes joins or is included alongside others already mentioned or implied. 'We' expands the circle of relevance, saying 'this one too belongs here.' It is the grammar of inclusion, of welcoming additional participants into whatever is being discussed.
sound_symbolism: The particle consists of 'w', the rounded glide that opens the mouth in welcome—the same sound that begins 'wiso' (reciprocal, each other), carrying connotations of mutuality and inclusion. This welcoming 'w' flows into 'e', the balanced mid vowel that adds without overwhelming, that joins without displacing. Speaking 'we' creates the acoustic gesture of opening to include: rounded welcome, balanced addition. The sound itself teaches that adding need not crowd out what was already present.
grammatical_notes: Occupies Slot 2, appearing immediately before the element being added. Indicates that the marked element is included alongside previously mentioned or contextually implied items. Can mark subjects, objects, or other constituents: 'we shia kela' (ko too rejoices), 'mia we nophi shelomu' (I understand the story too). Distinguished from coordinating conjunction 'nela' (and) which conjoins equals; 'we' marks addition to an existing set.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The interconnection of all things means nothing truly stands alone. 'We' acknowledges this by providing smooth grammar for addition, for recognizing that whatever we discuss always connects to more than first appears.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Inclusion is a foundation of peace. 'We' provides language for bringing others into consideration, for saying 'you also matter here.' This grammaticalizes the ethical practice of not forgetting anyone when benefits or attention are distributed.
